mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in real consumer credit growth indicates supply chain bottlenecks meeting persistent demand, which historically precedes profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During rapid rate hikes, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals tightening financial conditions that propagate to corporate borrowing costs, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative when HOUST declines during rate hikes, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.2 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts update consensus forecasts.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling correlation between GDP revisions and SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.0% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances following the inflation threshold breach, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while industrial production stagnates indicates a labor-driven demand surge that outpaces supply capacity, historically leading to margin pressure and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The employment-production divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of historical instances, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes (p > 0.15) in a multivariate regression controlling for sector weights.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, accelerating real consumer credit growth signals restoring household balance sheets and spending power, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite rebounds.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS accelerates during recovery periods, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
